raw_response: Based on the comprehensive review provided earlier, particularly focusing on how metoclopramide uniquely affects central dopaminergic systems leading to potential extrapyramidal side effects such as abnormal posturing, there isn't a compelling argument against selecting metoclopramide over the others. Each alternative medication either acts primarily peripherally without affecting neurotransmitter balance significantly (like dexamethasone), targets different receptor types where risk profiles differ considerably from those impacting movement control (ondansetron targeting serotonergic pathways; domperidone acting more selectively on peripheral dopaminergic sites).
